title: Battles of the U.S. Civil War

map.alt: Blank outline map of the United States with county lines

items.label: Fort Sumter
items.hint: The opening shots of the war, April 1861, in a Charleston, SC harbor.

items.label: Bull Run (Manassas)
items.hint: The first major land battle of the war, fought twice near a creek in northern Virginia.

items.label: Antietam
items.hint: The single bloodiest day in American military history, September 1862.

items.label: Gettysburg
items.hint: A three-day Pennsylvania battle often called the war's turning point.

items.label: Vicksburg
items.hint: A grueling siege on the Mississippi River that split the Confederacy in two.

items.label: Chickamauga
items.hint: A costly Confederate victory near Chattanooga, Tennessee.

items.label: Atlanta
items.hint: Its fall in 1864 helped secure Lincoln's re-election and preceded Sherman's March to the Sea.

items.label: Appomattox Court House
items.hint: Where Lee surrendered to Grant in April 1865, effectively ending the war.
